channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: SAP bets $1.16B on 18-month-old German AI lab and says yes to NemoClaw
item.description: SAP plans to buy German AI startup Prior Labs and invest heavily in it. It is also prohibiting customers' agents use to a select few like Nvidia's NemoClaw.

item.title: Miami startup Subquadratic claims 1,000x AI efficiency gain with SubQ model; researchers demand independent proof.
item.description: A little-known Miami-based startup called Subquadratic emerged from stealth on Tuesday with a sweeping claim: that it has built the first large language model to fully escape the mathematical constraint that has defined — and limited — every major AI system since 2017.The company claims its first model, SubQ 1M-Preview, is the first LLM built on a fully subquadratic architecture — one where compute grows linearly with context length. If that claim holds, it would be a genuine inflection point in how AI systems scale. At 12 million tokens, the company says, its architecture reduces attention co...

item.title: GPT-5.5 Instant shows you what it remembered — just not all of it
item.description: OpenAI updated the default model for ChatGPT to its new GPT-5.5 Instant, along with a new memory capability that finally shows which context shaped responses — at least some of them. This limitation signals that models are starting to create a second, incomplete memory observability layer that could conflict with existing audit systems and agent logs. GPT-5.5 Instant replaces GPT-5.3 Instant as the default ChatGPT model and is a version of its new flagship GPT-5.5 LLM. It’s supposed to be more dependable, accurate and smarter than 5.3. But it’s the introduction of memory sources, which will be...

item.title: ‘I Actually Thought He Was Going to Hit Me,’ OpenAI’s Greg Brockman Says of Elon Musk
item.description: OpenAI’s president wrapped his testimony on Tuesday by revealing a fiery meeting with Musk and subsequent efforts to remove several board members.

item.title: Bumble’s paying users are slipping as it bets on an overhaul later this year
item.description: The company is making a big bet that the swiping model is outdated and most matches never turn into actual dates. The company wants to fix that by redesigning profiles, changing how people interact, and focusing a lot more on getting users to meet in real life.

item.title: NYT Strands hints and answers for Wednesday, May 6 (game #794)
item.description: Looking for NYT Strands answers and hints? Here's all you need to know to solve today's game, including the spangram.

item.title: NYT Connections hints and answers for Wednesday, May 6 (game #1060)
item.description: Looking for NYT Connections answers and hints? Here's all you need to know to solve today's game, plus my commentary on the puzzles.

item.title: Quordle hints and answers for Wednesday, May 6 (game #1563)
item.description: Looking for Quordle clues? We can help. Plus get the answers to Quordle today and past solutions.

item.title: Google Gemini is coming to more cars, but some consumers aren’t cheering
item.description: Google Gemini is rolling out to millions of cars, but some owners are concerned about data privacy.

item.title: Altara secures $7M to bridge the data gap that’s slowing down physical sciences
item.description: Altara’s AI aims to diagnose failures and help speed up R&D by unifying data siloed across spreadsheets and legacy systems.

item.title: Promesses d’IA pour iPhone non tenues | Apple accepte de payer 250 millions de dollars US
item.description: Apple a accepté de verser 250 millions de dollars US pour régler un recours collectif l’accusant d’avoir trompé des millions d’acheteurs d’iPhone en vantant fin 2024 des capacités d’intelligence artificielle de son assistant vocal Siri qui n’existaient pas, selon l’accord soumis mardi à l’approbation de la justice.

item.title: Lucid Motors doesn’t know how many EVs it will build this year
item.description: Lucid Motors pulled its guidance for the year, as it navigates swelling inventory and a companywide cost-cutting measure.

item.title: 3D-printed Y-zipper turns from flexible to hard in less time than it takes to zip your fly
item.description: Researchers took a 40-year-old design and applied some software and 3D printing to bring the Y-zipper to life

item.title: Valve releases design files for its out-of-stock Steam Controller
item.description: Make and mod your own accessories if you've already got the gamepad.

item.title: How to watch The Other Bennet Sister from anywhere – it's *FREE*
item.description: Here's how to watch The Other Bennet Sister online from anywhere in the world, completely FREE.

item.title: 'Electricity with zero emission' — Chinese scientists aim to transform “dirty” coal with a new fuel cell that doesn't burn anything and produces clean power well beyond the Carnot cycle limit
item.description: Chinese researchers developed a coal fuel cell that generates electricity without combustion while capturing carbon dioxide and potentially achieving significantly higher efficiency levels.

item.title: One command turns any open-source repo into an AI agent backdoor. OpenClaw proved no supply-chain scanner has a detection category for it
item.description: Just two months ago, researchers at the Data Intelligence Lab at the University of Hong Kong introduced CLI-Anything, a new state-of-the-art tool that analyzes any repo’s source code and generates a structured command line interface (CLI) that AI coding agents can operate with a single command. Claude Code, Codex, OpenClaw, Cursor, and GitHub Copilot CLI are all supported, and since its launch in March, CLI‑Anything has climbed to more than 30,000 GitHub stars. But the same mechanism that makes software agent-native opens the door to agent-level poisoning. The attack community is already discu...
